    99	
   100	def _git_env_vars_to_strip() -> frozenset[str]:
   101	    """Return the union of static + locally-queried git-local vars.
   102	
   103	    Calls ``git rev-parse --local-env-vars`` once and caches the
   104	    result. Falls back to the static list if the subprocess fails
   105	    (e.g. git not installed, PATH issue). Either way, the returned
   106	    set is a strict superset of the static list so callers are never
   107	    worse off than without the dynamic query.
   108	    """
   109	    global _GIT_ENV_VARS_TO_STRIP_CACHE
   110	    if _GIT_ENV_VARS_TO_STRIP_CACHE is not None:
   111	        return _GIT_ENV_VARS_TO_STRIP_CACHE
   112	    dynamic: set[str] = set()
   113	    try:
   114	        result = subprocess.run(
   115	            ["git", "rev-parse", "--local-env-vars"],
   116	            capture_output=True,
   117	            text=True,
   118	            check=False,
   119	        )
   120	        if result.returncode == 0:
   121	            dynamic = {tok.strip() for tok in result.stdout.split() if tok.strip()}
   122	    except (OSError, FileNotFoundError):
   123	        pass
   124	    _GIT_ENV_VARS_TO_STRIP_CACHE = frozenset(_GIT_LOCAL_ENV_VARS_STATIC | dynamic)
   125	    return _GIT_ENV_VARS_TO_STRIP_CACHE
   126	
   127	
   128	# Back-compat alias: existing tests reference this name.
   129	_GIT_ENV_VARS_TO_STRIP = _GIT_LOCAL_ENV_VARS_STATIC

   166	class GitRepo:

   169	    def __init__(self, path: Path | str) -> None:
   170	        self.path = Path(path).resolve()

   172	
   173	    # --- factories --------------------------------------------------------
   174	
   175	    @classmethod
   176	    def init_bare(cls, path: Path | str) -> GitRepo:
   177	        """Initialize a new bare repository at ``path`` and return a repo.
   178	
   179	        Runs under a sanitized environment (``GIT_CONFIG_NOSYSTEM=1``,
   180	        ``GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL=/dev/null``, ``--template=``) so the repo's
   181	        initial state is deterministic regardless of the user's
   182	        ambient git config or template dir.
   183	        """
   184	        target = Path(path)
   185	        target.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   186	        _run_git(
   187	            ["init", "--bare", "--initial-branch=main", "--template=", str(target)],
   188	            cwd=target.parent,
   189	        )
   190	        return cls(target)
   191	
   192	    @classmethod
   193	    def init(cls, path: Path | str) -> GitRepo:
   194	        """Initialize a new non-bare repository at ``path`` and return a repo.
   195	
   196	        Sanitized per :meth:`init_bare`.
   197	        """
   198	        target = Path(path)
   199	        target.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   200	        _run_git(
   201	            ["init", "--initial-branch=main", "--template=", str(target)],
   202	            cwd=target.parent,
   203	        )
   204	        return cls(target)
   205	
   206	    # --- introspection ----------------------------------------------------
   207	
   208	    def is_bare(self) -> bool:
   209	        """Return whether the repository is bare."""
   210	        result = self._run(["rev-parse", "--is-bare-repository"])
   211	        return result.stdout.strip() == "true"
   212	
   213	    def rev_parse(self, ref_or_sha: str) -> str:
   214	        """Resolve a ref or SHA prefix to a full 40-character SHA."""
   215	        result = self._run(["rev-parse", "--verify", f"{ref_or_sha}^{{commit}}"])
   216	        return result.stdout.strip()
   217	
   218	    def commit_exists(self, sha: str) -> bool:
   219	        """Return whether ``sha`` names a commit object in the repository."""
   220	        result = self._run(["cat-file", "-t", sha], check=False)
   221	        return result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip() == "commit"
   222	
   223	    def ref_exists(self, refname: str) -> bool:
   224	        """Return whether a ref exists (e.g. ``refs/heads/trial/t1-slug``)."""
   225	        result = self._run(["show-ref", "--verify", "--quiet", refname], check=False)
   226	        return result.returncode == 0
   227	
   228	    def resolve_ref(self, refname: str) -> str | None:
   229	        """Return the SHA a ref points to, or ``None`` if the ref is absent."""
   230	        result = self._run(["show-ref", "--verify", refname], check=False)
   231	        if result.returncode != 0:
   232	            return None
   233	        line = result.stdout.strip().split()
   234	        return line[0] if line else None
   235	
   236	    def list_refs(self, pattern: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
   237	        """Return ``(refname, sha)`` pairs matching a ``for-each-ref`` pattern."""
   238	        result = self._run(
   239	            ["for-each-ref", "--format=%(refname) %(objectname)", pattern],
   240	        )

   242	        for line in result.stdout.splitlines():
   243	            line = line.strip()
   244	            if not line:
   245	                continue
   246	            name, _, sha = line.partition(" ")
   247	            pairs.append((name, sha))

   249	
   250	    def is_ancestor(self, maybe_ancestor: str, descendant: str) -> bool:

     2	
     3	Every EDEN store backend — in-memory, SQLite, a future Postgres or
     4	remote backend — has to enforce the same state-machine, the same
     5	composite commits (``spec/v0/05-event-protocol.md`` §2.2), the same
     6	token/idempotency/terminal-immutability rules, and the same metrics
     7	validation. Factoring that logic out of any one backend and sharing
     8	it is the only way to make "passes the same conformance suite"
     9	mean what it says.
    10	
    11	``_StoreBase`` owns:
    12	
    13	- The public method surface (``claim``, ``submit``, ``accept``,
    14	  ``reject``, ``reclaim``, ``create_*``, ``read_*``, ``list_*``,
    15	  ``events``, ``validate_acceptance``, ``validate_terminal``,
    16	  ``create_proposal``, ``mark_proposal_ready``, ``create_trial``,
    17	  ``declare_trial_eval_error``, ``integrate_trial``).
    18	- All validation, composite-commit staging, and event construction.
    19	
    20	Subclasses own:
    21	
    22	- ``_atomic_operation`` — the transaction scope. In-memory wraps an
    23	  ``RLock``; SQLite wraps ``BEGIN IMMEDIATE``…``COMMIT``.
    24	- ``_get_task``/``_get_proposal``/``_get_trial``/``_get_submission``
    25	  — primitive lookups.
    26	- ``_iter_tasks``/``_iter_proposals``/``_iter_trials``/``_iter_events``
    27	  — ordered iteration.
    28	- ``_apply_commit(tx)`` — apply the staged ``_Tx`` inside the already-
    29	  open transaction, without committing it. The outer
    30	  ``_atomic_operation`` context does the actual commit.
    31	
    32	Every public method follows the same pattern: open an atomic
    33	operation, perform reads + validations (which may raise before any
    34	write), stage all writes into a ``_Tx`` object, and call
    35	``self._apply_commit(tx)`` exactly once. If validation raises the
    36	atomic operation aborts and no partial state becomes visible
    37	(chapter 8 §6.1–§6.3).

   113	def _validated_update[M: BaseModel](model: M, **changes: Any) -> M:
   114	    """Return a copy of ``model`` with ``changes`` applied and re-validated.
   115	
   116	    Replaces Pydantic's ``model_copy(update=...)``, which does **not**
   117	    re-run validators. Without re-validation a caller could stamp an
   118	    invalid ``commit_sha``, ``artifacts_uri``, or ``metrics`` shape
   119	    onto a stored trial. Re-validating on every update is how every
   120	    backend honors ``03-roles.md`` §3.4, §4.4 and ``08-storage.md``
   121	    §3.
   122	
   123	    Passing ``None`` for a field removes it (matches the ``NotNone``
   124	    rule on optional typed fields in ``eden_contracts._common``:
   125	    absent is permitted, explicit null is not).
   126	    """
   127	    data = model.model_dump(mode="json", exclude_none=True)
   128	    for key, value in changes.items():
   129	        if value is None:
   130	            data.pop(key, None)
   131	        elif isinstance(value, BaseModel):
   132	            data[key] = value.model_dump(mode="json", exclude_none=True)
   133	        else:
   134	            data[key] = value

    43	    evaluate_task_id_factory: Callable[[], str],
    44	    integrator_commit_factory: Callable[[str], str] | None = None,
    45	) -> None:
    46	    """Drive an experiment to quiescence through the three workers.
    47	
    48	    Creates each ``plan_task_id`` as a plan task, then loops: run
    49	    planner → orchestrator finalizes plan submissions → dispatch
    50	    implement tasks → run implementer → finalize → dispatch evaluate
    51	    tasks → run evaluator → finalize. The loop terminates when a full
    52	    pass over every role and every orchestrator step produces no
    53	    progress. If ``integrator_commit_factory`` is supplied, any trial
    54	    that reaches ``success`` with no ``trial_commit_sha`` yet is
    55	    promoted at the end of each pass.
    56	    """

    61	        progress = False
    62	        progress |= planner.run_pending(store) > 0
    63	        progress |= _finalize_submitted(store, kind="plan")
    64	        progress |= _dispatch_implement_tasks(store, implement_task_id_factory)
    65	        progress |= implementer.run_pending(store) > 0
    66	        progress |= _finalize_submitted(store, kind="implement")
    67	        progress |= _dispatch_evaluate_tasks(store, evaluate_task_id_factory)
    68	        progress |= evaluator.run_pending(store) > 0
    69	        progress |= _finalize_submitted(store, kind="evaluate")

    71	            progress |= _promote_successful_trials(store, integrator_commit_factory)

    76	def _finalize_submitted(store: Store, *, kind: str) -> bool:
    77	    """Accept/reject every submitted task of ``kind``.
    78	
    79	    Uses ``store.validate_terminal`` to pick the right terminal
    80	    transition: accept, reject(worker_error), or reject(validation_error).
    81	    Malformed payloads on either success or error submissions surface
    82	    as ``validation_error`` rather than propagating as exceptions, so
    83	    the task always reaches a terminal state (``04-task-protocol.md``
    84	    §4.3).
    85	    """
    86	    progress = False
    87	    for task in store.list_tasks(kind=kind, state="submitted"):
    88	        decision, _reason = store.validate_terminal(task.task_id)
    89	        if decision == "accept":
    90	            store.accept(task.task_id)
    91	        elif decision == "reject_worker":
    92	            store.reject(task.task_id, "worker_error")
    93	        else:
    94	            store.reject(task.task_id, "validation_error")

    96	    return progress

    99	def _dispatch_implement_tasks(

   110	def _dispatch_evaluate_tasks(

    92	  `dispatched` ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §2).
    93	  Events: `task.created` + `proposal.dispatched`, in one atomic
    94	  commit.
    95	- **Implement terminal** — the `implement` task's terminal transition
    96	  (`submitted → completed` or `submitted → failed`) and the matching
    97	  `proposal` transition from `dispatched` to `completed`
    98	  ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3, §7). Events:
    99	  `task.completed` (or `task.failed`) + `proposal.completed`, in one
   100	  atomic commit.
   101	- **Evaluate terminal (`success`/`error`)** — the `evaluate` task's
   102	  terminal transition plus writes to the trial's `status`, `metrics`,
   103	  `artifacts_uri`, and `completed_at` ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md)
   104	  §4.4). Events: `task.completed` (or `task.failed`) +
   105	  `trial.succeeded` / `trial.errored`, in one atomic commit.
   106	- **Implement reclaim with in-flight trial** — reclamation of an
   107	  `implement` task whose prior execution left a trial in `starting`
   108	  requires transitioning that trial to `error` atomically with the
   109	  reclaim ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §5.4). Events:
   110	  `task.reclaimed` + `trial.errored`, in one atomic commit.
   111	- **Retry-exhausted `eval_error` terminal** — the orchestrator's
   112	  transition of a trial from `starting` to `eval_error`
   113	  ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3). When the
   114	  orchestrator persists this transition as a state change on the
   115	  trial, it MUST emit `trial.eval_errored` atomically.
   116	- **Trial promotion** — the integrator's write of a `trial/*` commit
   117	  and the `trial_commit_sha` field on the trial
   118	  ([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md)). Event:
   119	  `trial.integrated`.
   120	
   121	A subscriber processing any of these composite events MUST therefore
   122	either observe the full set or observe none; partial visibility is a
   123	protocol violation.
   124	
   125	### 2.3 What is *not* a state change
   126	
   127	Operations that do not change protocol-owned state do not require
   128	events:
   129	
   130	- Reading a task, proposal, trial, or event.
   131	- Uploading artifacts to the artifact store before they are referenced
   132	  from a protocol-owned object. (Populating `artifacts_uri` on a
   133	  proposal or trial *is* a state change and is covered by the events
   134	  on those objects.)
   135	- Worker-internal progress (scratch files, local logs).
   136	
   137	Implementations MAY expose operational telemetry through other
   138	channels without running it through the event log.
   139	
   140	## 3. Event registry
   141	
   142	Every v0 event `type` defined by the protocol is listed below. For
   143	each, the `data` object's required fields are pinned; optional fields
   144	are called out per entry. The JSON Schema in
   145	[`schemas/event.schema.json`](schemas/event.schema.json) enforces
   146	these shapes via `if/then` dispatch on `type`.
   147	
   148	Implementations MAY emit additional event types outside this registry
   149	(§3.5). The `type` pattern in §1 is the only structural constraint on
   150	them.
   151	
   152	### 3.1 Task events
   153	
   154	Produced atomically with the transitions defined in
   155	[`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md). The `task_id` field on
   156	every task-event payload names the task whose transition is being
   157	recorded.
   158	
   159	| Type | Transition | `data` required fields |
   160	|---|---|---|
   161	| `task.created` | — → `pending` | `task_id`, `kind` |
   162	| `task.claimed` | `pending` → `claimed` | `task_id`, `worker_id` |
   163	| `task.submitted` | `claimed` → `submitted` | `task_id` |
   164	| `task.completed` | `submitted` → `completed` | `task_id` |
   165	| `task.failed` | `submitted` → `failed` | `task_id`, `reason` |
   166	| `task.reclaimed` | `claimed`/`submitted` → `pending` | `task_id`, `cause` |
   167	
   168	Payload field definitions:
   169	
   170	- `task_id` — the `task_id` of the transitioning task.
   171	- `kind` — the task's `kind`
   172	  ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §3.1); one of `plan`,
   173	  `implement`, `evaluate`.
   174	- `worker_id` — the `claim.worker_id` recorded on the successful
   175	  claim ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §3.2).
   176	- `reason` — one of the strings `"worker_error"` (the worker's
   177	  submission declared failure), `"validation_error"` (the orchestrator
   178	  rejected the result as malformed or non-conforming), or
   179	  `"policy_limit"` (a policy such as retry budget caused the failure).
   180	  The literal set is closed for v0; an implementation that needs
   181	  finer granularity MAY add a separate operator-level event under its
   182	  own type (§3.5).
   183	- `cause` — one of `"expired"` (claim `expires_at` passed),
   184	  `"operator"` (explicit operator action), or `"health_policy"`
   185	  (task store health policy declared the worker unreachable). The
   186	  literal set is closed for v0 on the same terms as `reason`.
   187	
   188	The payload MAY include additional fields beyond those required;
   189	subscribers MUST tolerate them. A conforming orchestrator SHOULD
   190	include the submitting worker's `worker_id` on `task.submitted`,
   191	`task.completed`, and `task.failed` events when known, as an
   192	operational convenience — but this is not required because the
   193	worker-task binding is already recoverable from the preceding
   194	`task.claimed` event.
   195	
   196	### 3.2 Proposal events
   197	
   198	Produced atomically with the proposal `state` transitions defined in
   199	[`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §5 and
   200	[`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §2.2. Composite commits that bind a
   201	proposal event to a task event are enumerated in §2.2.
   202	
   203	| Type | Transition | `data` required fields |
   204	|---|---|---|
   205	| `proposal.drafted` | — → `drafting` | `proposal_id` |
   206	| `proposal.ready` | `drafting` → `ready` | `proposal_id` |
   207	| `proposal.dispatched` | `ready` → `dispatched` | `proposal_id`, `task_id` |
   208	| `proposal.completed` | `dispatched` → `completed` | `proposal_id`, `task_id` |
   209	
   210	Payload field definitions:
   211	
   212	- `proposal_id` — the `proposal_id` of the transitioning proposal.
   213	- `task_id` — for `proposal.dispatched`, the `task_id` of the
   214	  `implement` task created by the same commit (§2.2). For
   215	  `proposal.completed`, the `task_id` of the `implement` task whose
   216	  terminal transition is being recorded.
   217	
   218	### 3.3 Trial events
   219	
   220	Produced atomically with trial `status` transitions and with the
   221	integrator's promotion write. The trial's lifecycle is defined in
   222	[`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7 and
   223	[`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §3–§4; the promotion step is in
   224	[`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md).
   225	
   226	| Type | Transition | `data` required fields |
   227	|---|---|---|
   228	| `trial.started` | — → `starting` | `trial_id`, `proposal_id` |
   229	| `trial.succeeded` | `starting` → `success` | `trial_id`, `commit_sha` |
   230	| `trial.errored` | `starting` → `error` | `trial_id` |
   231	| `trial.eval_errored` | `starting` → `eval_error` | `trial_id` |
   232	| `trial.integrated` | — (integrator writes `trial_commit_sha`) | `trial_id`, `trial_commit_sha` |
   233	
   234	Payload field definitions:
   235	
   236	- `trial_id` — the `trial_id` of the transitioning trial.
   237	- `proposal_id` — the trial's `proposal_id`
   238	  ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1).
   239	- `commit_sha` — the worker-branch tip recorded on the trial at the
   240	  moment of success ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1).
   241	- `trial_commit_sha` — the canonical-lineage SHA the integrator wrote
   242	  ([`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md)). A 40-hex SHA-1 or a
   243	  64-hex SHA-256; the same pattern as commits elsewhere in the data
   244	  model.
   245	
   246	`trial.integrated` is not a trial-`status` transition — integration
   247	does not change the trial's `status` field, only its
   248	`trial_commit_sha`. The event marks promotion so subscribers can
   249	reconstruct the canonical lineage without reading git directly.
   250	
   251	### 3.4 Subscriber observability guarantee
   252	
   253	Every protocol-owned state-machine transition in v0 is covered by
   254	exactly one event type in §3.1–§3.3. A subscriber that consumes
   255	every event with a registered `type`, in log order, MUST be able to
   256	reconstruct the **lifecycle history** of every task, proposal, and
   257	trial in the experiment: which entities exist, in what states, in
   258	what order. A conforming implementation MUST NOT expose a state-
   259	machine transition that is not marked by its corresponding
   260	registered event.
   261	
   262	Registered event payloads carry only the fields needed to *identify*
   263	the transitioning entity and any cross-entity references (e.g.
   264	`proposal.dispatched` carries the implement `task_id`). They do
   265	not carry full entity snapshots. A subscriber that needs the content
   266	of an entity (the proposal's `parent_commits` and `artifacts_uri`,
   267	the trial's `metrics` and `completed_at`) MUST read that entity from
   268	its store using the identifier the event carries; the read returns
   269	the entity's current state
   270	([`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md) §1.7). This boundary is deliberate:
   271	events mark what happened; the entity stores hold what the entity
   272	*is*. Coupling the two into a full event-sourced projection — a
   273	subscriber reconstructing every intermediate entity value from
   274	events alone — is a deployment MAY implement on top of v0 but is not
   275	a protocol requirement, since v0 does not mandate historical reads
   276	on the entity stores.
   277	
   278	The transactional invariant (§2) combined with the atomic event +
   279	state-change rule guarantees that any entity reachable via an event's
   280	identifier is already durable at read time.

1	# Integrator
     2	
     3	This chapter specifies the integrator role in full: the git topology
     4	it operates on, the promotion trigger, the squash rule, the
     5	eval-manifest shape, and the invariants it preserves across the
     6	canonical trial lineage.
     7	
     8	The integrator is introduced in [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §5,
     9	which pins its boundary rules — exclusive authority over `trial/*`,
    10	exclusive authority over `trial_commit_sha`. This chapter specifies
    11	what the integrator *produces* inside those boundaries.
    12	
    13	The three-namespace topology (`main`, `trial/*`, `work/*`) is defined
    14	in [`01-concepts.md`](01-concepts.md) §9. This chapter pins the
    15	invariants on each namespace and the transformation from worker
    16	branch to canonical trial commit.
    17	
    18	## 1. Topology invariants
    19	
    20	A conforming EDEN deployment operates on a single git repository per
    21	experiment. That repository carries three ref namespaces:
    22	
    23	### 1.1 `main`
    24	
    25	- Names the experiment's starting commit.
    26	- MUST be present at experiment registration time and MUST name a
    27	  commit reachable by every proposal's `parent_commits`
    28	  ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §5.2).
    29	- Is **immutable** for the duration of the experiment. A conforming
    30	  integrator MUST NOT move, rewrite, or delete `main`; neither MAY
    31	  any worker. External tooling (e.g. repo maintainers merging work
    32	  into `main` outside the experiment) is the operator's concern and
    33	  lies outside the protocol. An experiment MAY tolerate such
    34	  external moves only if it does not depend on `main` as a
    35	  persistent parent; conforming implementations SHOULD record the
    36	  experiment's starting commit SHA explicitly and use that SHA, not
    37	  the current `main`, when validating parent reachability.
    38	
    39	### 1.2 `trial/*`
    40	
    41	- Holds the canonical trial lineage, one commit per integrated trial.
    42	- The integrator is the **sole writer** ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md)
    43	  §5.1). No other role — planner, implementer, evaluator,
    44	  orchestrator, operator tooling invoked through protocol-owned
    45	  channels — MAY write to `trial/*`.
    46	- Each `trial/*` branch MUST name exactly one trial. Branch naming
    47	  conforms to §3.1.
    48	- A `trial/*` branch MUST NOT be deleted or rewritten once written.
    49	  If an integrator discovers a bug in an earlier promotion, it MAY
    50	  record corrective state in subsequent events or operator-level
    51	  channels, but MUST NOT rewrite history on `trial/*`.
    52	
    53	### 1.3 `work/*`
    54	
    55	- Holds per-trial worker branches written by implementers
    56	  ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §3.3).
    57	- Each `work/*` branch MUST be unique to a single trial. A trial's
    58	  `branch` field ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1)
    59	  records the exact branch name.
    60	- Worker branches are **inputs** to the integrator, not normative
    61	  outputs. A conforming deployment MAY retain them for audit and
    62	  debugging after promotion, MAY garbage-collect them after a
    63	  retention window, and MAY delete them eagerly once a trial is
    64	  integrated — the retention policy is a deployment concern. No
    65	  protocol-owned reader MAY depend on a worker branch after the
    66	  trial's promotion.
    67	- The evaluator reads a worker branch at the trial's `commit_sha`
    68	  during evaluation ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §4.1); the
    69	  implementer's read-your-writes guarantee applies only until
    70	  promotion, after which the worker branch's reachability is no
    71	  longer a protocol-owned concern.
    72	
    73	### 1.4 Reachability rule
    74	
    75	Every commit on a `work/*` branch MUST descend from the proposal's
    76	declared `parent_commits` ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §3.3). The
    77	integrator MUST reject a trial whose `commit_sha` does not satisfy
    78	this rule; rejection produces no `trial/*` commit and no
    79	`trial.integrated` event. The orchestrator MAY transition the trial
    80	to `error` via the normal channels if a reachability violation is
    81	discovered, but the integrator MUST NOT fabricate a `trial.errored`
    82	event itself — events are paired with state changes, and the
    83	integrator is not the writer for trial `status`.
    84	
    85	## 2. Promotion trigger
    86	
    87	The integrator promotes a trial iff:
    88	
    89	- The trial's `status` is `success`
    90	  ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1).
    91	- The trial's `commit_sha` is set and resolves to a commit on the
    92	  trial's `branch` in the experiment repository.
    93	- The trial has not yet been promoted (`trial_commit_sha` is absent).
    94	
    95	The mechanism by which the integrator observes the trigger —
    96	subscribing to `trial.succeeded` events, polling the trial store, or
    97	receiving a dispatch call — is a binding concern and is not pinned.
    98	[`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §5.2.
    99	
   100	A conforming integrator MUST NOT promote trials in any other status.
   101	In particular, `error`, `eval_error`, and `starting` trials MUST NOT
   102	receive a `trial/*` commit.
   103	
   104	The integrator MUST NOT promote a trial whose `metrics` do not
   105	validate against the experiment's `metrics_schema`
   106	([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.2,
   107	[`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md) §4). The orchestrator's acceptance
   108	of a `success` submission is the primary guard for this; the
   109	integrator MAY additionally re-validate as defense in depth but MUST
   110	NOT silently drop or coerce invalid metrics.
   111	
   112	## 3. Promotion output
   113	
   114	### 3.1 `trial/*` branch name
   115	
   116	The integrator creates a branch under `trial/` named:
   117	
   118	```text
   119	trial/<trial_id>-<slug>
   120	```
   121	
   122	where `<trial_id>` is the trial's `trial_id`
   123	([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1) and `<slug>` is the
   124	`slug` of the trial's parent proposal
   125	([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §5.1). The slug is already
   126	constrained to `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$`, and `trial_id` is opaque; the
   127	resulting branch name MUST be valid under git's ref-format rules
   128	([git check-ref-format]). If an implementation's `trial_id` choice
   129	could violate ref-format rules, the implementation MUST choose a
   130	different `trial_id` representation — it MUST NOT mutate the stored
   131	`trial_id` in the trial object to accommodate its branch-naming
   132	scheme.
   133	
   134	[git check-ref-format]: https://git-scm.com/docs/git-check-ref-format
   135	
   136	### 3.2 Squash rule
   137	
   138	The trial's worker branch MAY contain multiple commits (implementer
   139	step-by-step work, partial progress, iterated attempts). The
   140	canonical trial commit MUST be a **single commit** whose tree is the
   141	worker branch's tip tree plus exactly the eval-manifest file.
   142	
   143	Concretely: the integrator MUST produce a commit `T` with:
   144	
   145	- `tree(T)` equal to `tree(commit_sha)` with exactly one path added:
   146	  the eval-manifest file at the path given in §4.1. The added file's
   147	  bytes MUST be the manifest defined by §4.2. `commit_sha` is the
   148	  trial's recorded worker-branch tip. No other path — not a file,
   149	  not a directory entry — MAY be added, removed, or modified in the
   150	  squash.
   151	- `parents(T) == <parent_commits>` of the trial, in the order
   152	  recorded on the trial
   153	  ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1 inherits from the
   154	  proposal, §5.1).
   155	- A commit message whose first line (subject) records the trial
   156	  identity in a machine-parseable form. §3.3 pins the format.
   157	
   158	The implementer is not required to carry the eval manifest in its
   159	worker branch; the manifest is exclusively the integrator's write.
   160	If a worker branch incidentally contains a file at the eval-manifest
   161	path (e.g. an implementer mistake), the integrator MUST reject the
   162	trial rather than silently overwrite the file. Rejection produces
   163	no `trial/*` commit and no `trial.integrated` event; the orchestrator
   164	MAY transition the trial via normal channels.
   165	
   166	The squash is a single merge-commit-shaped object (parents may be
   167	one or many, per the proposal's `parent_commits`); intermediate
   168	work-branch commits MUST NOT appear in `T`'s ancestor chain along a
   169	first-parent path when read from `trial/*`. Readers of the
   170	canonical lineage see one commit per trial, not the worker's
   171	intermediate history.
   172	
   173	A conforming implementation MAY additionally preserve the worker
   174	branch's history elsewhere (retained `work/*` ref, archive) for
   175	audit, but that preservation MUST NOT make intermediate commits
   176	reachable from `trial/*`.
   177	
   178	### 3.3 Commit message
   179	
   180	The `trial/*` commit message's first line (subject) MUST match:
   181	
   182	```text
   183	trial: <trial_id> <slug>
   184	```
   185	
   186	The subject MUST carry both the `trial_id` and the parent proposal's
   187	`slug`, separated by a single space. The body MAY include additional
   188	human-readable context. The machine-parseable header allows a
   189	subscriber reading git alone — without the event log — to recover the
   190	`trial_id` of any `trial/*` commit.
   191	
   192	### 3.4 Atomic write
   193	
   194	The promotion MUST be atomic with two other state changes:
   195	
   196	1. Writing the `trial_commit_sha` field on the trial to the SHA of
   197	   the new `trial/*` commit
   198	   ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1).
   199	2. Appending a `trial.integrated` event to the event log
   200	   ([`05-event-protocol.md`](05-event-protocol.md) §3.3).
   201	
   202	If any of the three steps fails — git ref write, trial-object
   203	field write, event append — the integrator MUST roll back any
   204	already-performed step. In particular, a dangling `trial/*` ref with
   205	no `trial_commit_sha` field and no `trial.integrated` event is a
   206	protocol violation. Implementations MAY use store-level
   207	transactions, outbox patterns, compensating deletes, or any other
   208	mechanism; the observable invariant is that a reader of any one of
   209	the three artifacts (ref, field, event) MUST observe the other two.
   210	
   211	## 4. Eval manifest
   212	
   213	Each `trial/*` commit MUST carry an **eval manifest** at a fixed path
   214	inside its tree. The manifest is the canonical, git-embedded record
   215	of the trial's evaluator outputs: what was measured, which worker
   216	commit was measured, where the supporting artifacts live.
   217	
   218	### 4.1 Path
   219	
   220	The eval manifest is a JSON file at:
   221	
   222	```text
   223	.eden/trials/<trial_id>/eval.json
   224	```
   225	
   226	relative to the root of the committed tree. The `<trial_id>`
   227	component matches the trial's `trial_id`. An integrator MUST NOT add
   228	any file under `.eden/trials/<other_trial_id>/` to a trial's commit;
   229	each `trial/*` commit carries exactly one eval-manifest path,
   230	corresponding to its own trial.
   231	
   232	### 4.2 Shape
   233	
   234	The manifest is a JSON object with the following required fields.
   235	Each required field is taken from state the protocol already writes
   236	via the role contracts in [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) — no role
   237	submission is extended by this chapter.
   238	
   239	| Field | Type | Source |
   240	|---|---|---|
   241	| `trial_id` | string | The trial's `trial_id` ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1). |
   242	| `proposal_id` | string | The trial's `proposal_id` ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1). |
   243	| `commit_sha` | string | The worker-branch tip the evaluator measured ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1). |
   244	| `parent_commits` | array of string | The trial's `parent_commits`, in order ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1). |
   245	| `metrics` | object | The evaluator's metrics payload ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §4.4), conforming to the experiment's `metrics_schema`. |
   246	| `completed_at` | timestamp | The trial's `completed_at` ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7.1). UTC, RFC 3339 profile as elsewhere in the data model. |
   247	
   248	Optional fields:
   249	
   250	| Field | Type | Source |
   251	|---|---|---|
   252	| `artifacts_uri` | string (URI) | The trial's `artifacts_uri` if the evaluator supplied one ([`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §4.4). |
   253	| `description` | string | The trial's `description`, copied from the trial object if present. |
   254	| `evaluator` | object | Implementation-defined identity of the evaluator (§4.3). Present when the evaluator's role-binding surfaces this metadata. |
   255	| `artifacts` | object | Implementation-defined inventory of per-file artifact metadata (§4.4). Present when the role-binding surfaces it. |
   256	
   257	Every required field's value MUST equal the corresponding field on
   258	the trial object at the moment of promotion. A conforming integrator
   259	MUST NOT synthesize or transform required-field values; they are a
   260	canonical, in-tree snapshot of the trial state, not a separate

150	  empty.
   151	- `status` — one of `"success"` (the planner completed normally,
   152	  regardless of proposal count) or `"error"` (the planner could
   153	  not complete; any partially-written proposals MUST remain in
   154	  `"drafting"` state and MUST NOT be dispatched).
   155	
   156	A planner MUST NOT submit a `plan` task while any of its
   157	referenced proposals is still in `"drafting"` state.
   158	
   159	## 3. Implementer
   160	
   161	The implementer realizes a proposal as a working-tree change on a
   162	per-trial branch.
   163	
   164	### 3.1 Inputs
   165	
   166	An implementer receives:
   167	
   168	- The task object (`kind == "implement"`,
   169	  `payload.proposal_id == P`).
   170	- Read access to the proposal `P`. The orchestrator MUST ensure
   171	  `P.state == "ready"` before dispatch.
   172	- Read and write access to the git repository associated with
   173	  `P.experiment_id`, at a revision reachable from
   174	  `P.parent_commits`. How the repository is located and how
   175	  access is granted is a role-binding concern (§6,
   176	  [`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §2.4); the protocol
   177	  constrains only that the implementer's writes land under
   178	  `work/*` and that no commit is introduced whose history does
   179	  not descend from `P.parent_commits` (§3.3).
   180	- Read access to the artifacts at `P.artifacts_uri`.
   181	
   182	### 3.2 Outputs
   183	
   184	The implementer produces a **trial** object
   185	([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §7). It MUST:
   186	
   187	1. Persist a trial with `status == "starting"`,
   188	   `experiment_id == P.experiment_id`, `proposal_id == P`, and
   189	   `parent_commits == P.parent_commits`, before making any
   190	   repository write observable to other roles.
   191	2. Write the implementation on a branch under `work/*`
   192	   ([`01-concepts.md`](01-concepts.md) §9) whose parent(s) are the
   193	   proposal's `parent_commits`. Set the trial's `branch` field to
   194	   this branch name.
   195	3. On successful completion, set the trial's `commit_sha` to the
   196	   tip of the `work/*` branch. Optionally set `artifacts_uri` and
   197	   `description`.
   198	
   199	The implementer MUST NOT write to `trial/*` or `main`; those are
   200	owned by the integrator. The `trial_commit_sha` field on the trial
   201	MUST NOT be set by the implementer.
   202	
   203	### 3.3 Worker-branch invariants
   204	
   205	- The `work/*` branch MUST be unique to this trial. Two trials
   206	  MUST NOT share a worker branch name.
   207	- Every commit on the worker branch MUST be reachable from the
   208	  declared `parent_commits`. An implementer MUST NOT introduce
   209	  commits whose history does not descend from the proposal's
   210	  declared parents.
   211	- The implementer MAY produce multiple commits on the worker
   212	  branch. The evaluator consumes only the tip (`commit_sha`); the
   213	  integrator's squash rule is defined in
   214	  [`06-integrator.md`](06-integrator.md).
   215	
   216	### 3.4 Submission
   217	
   218	The implementer submits with:
   219	
   220	- `trial_id` — the trial it created.
   221	- `status` — one of:
   222	  - `"success"` — the `work/*` branch tip is `commit_sha` and is
   223	    ready to be evaluated.
   224	  - `"error"` — the implementer could not realize the proposal.
   225	    The trial MUST be persisted with `status == "error"`. No
   226	    evaluate task is dispatched against an errored trial.
   227	- `commit_sha` — required when `status == "success"`; MUST equal
   228	  the tip of the worker branch.
   229	
   230	A resubmission of the same `implement` task MUST be idempotent: a
   231	duplicate submit presenting the same `trial_id` and `commit_sha`
   232	MUST be accepted without side effect. A duplicate submit that
   233	disagrees with the already-recorded result MUST be rejected
   234	([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.2).
   235	
   236	## 4. Evaluator
   237	
   238	The evaluator measures a completed trial against the experiment's
   239	metrics schema.
   240	
   241	### 4.1 Inputs
   242	
   243	An evaluator receives:
   244	
   245	- The task object (`kind == "evaluate"`,
   246	  `payload.trial_id == T`).
   247	- Read access to the trial `T`. The orchestrator MUST ensure
   248	  `T.status == "starting"` and `T.commit_sha` is set before
   249	  dispatch.
   250	- Read access to the git repository at `T.commit_sha` on the
   251	  worker branch (`T.branch`). Repository location and access are
   252	  a role-binding concern, as for the implementer (§3.1).
   253	- Read access to the experiment's `metrics_schema` and
   254	  `objective`.
   255	
   256	### 4.2 Outputs
   257	
   258	The evaluator MUST:
   259	
   260	1. Produce a `metrics` object whose keys are a subset of the
   261	   declared `metrics_schema` keys and whose values satisfy the
   262	   per-metric type rules
   263	   ([`02-data-model.md`](02-data-model.md) §1.3, §7.2).
   264	2. Optionally upload supporting artifacts (logs, captured outputs,
   265	   diagnostic files).
   266	
   267	The evaluator MUST NOT modify the worker branch or any
   268	protocol-owned mutable state other than the trial fields the
   269	submission writes (§4.4) and the task it holds a claim on. In
   270	particular, the evaluator MUST NOT write to the trial's
   271	`completed_at`, `metrics`, `artifacts_uri`, `description`, or
   272	`status` directly; those writes are performed by the orchestrator
   273	when the submitted task reaches its terminal state (§4.4,
   274	[`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3).
   275	
   276	### 4.3 Non-interference
   277	
   278	The evaluator reads the repository at the trial's worker-branch tip
   279	but MUST NOT push, rewrite, or delete that branch, and MUST NOT
   280	write to the canonical trial lineage. Any side effects of evaluation
   281	(test caches, build outputs, captured logs) MUST NOT be observable
   282	through any protocol-owned store, ref, or artifact location except
   283	via the evaluator's declared outputs (§4.2).
   284	
   285	### 4.4 Submission
   286	
   287	The evaluator submits with:
   288	
   289	- `trial_id` — the trial it evaluated.
   290	- `status` — one of:
   291	  - `"success"` — the trial ran and produced metrics.
   292	  - `"error"` — the trial could not be evaluated for reasons
   293	    attributable to the trial's own code (build failure, test
   294	    failure, etc.). The evaluator MAY still include partial
   295	    metrics.
   296	  - `"eval_error"` — the evaluator itself failed for reasons
   297	    unrelated to the trial's code (infrastructure fault,
   298	    evaluator bug). While a fresh evaluate task MAY still be
   299	    created for this trial, the trial's status MUST remain
   300	    `"starting"`. If the orchestrator's retry policy is
   301	    exhausted (or the operator abandons evaluation), the
   302	    orchestrator MUST transition the trial's status to
   303	    `"eval_error"`, making that status terminal for the trial
   304	    ([`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3).
   305	- `metrics` — the metrics object described in §4.2. MAY be absent
   306	  when `status == "eval_error"`.
   307	- `artifacts_uri` — OPTIONAL. A URI the evaluator uploaded
   308	  supporting artifacts to.
   309	
   310	On a `submitted → completed` or `submitted → failed` transition
   311	(per [`04-task-protocol.md`](04-task-protocol.md) §4.3), the
   312	orchestrator MUST write the following trial fields atomically with
   313	the event:
   314	
   315	- `status` — the trial status implied by the submission: `"success"`
   316	  when the submission's `status == "success"`; `"error"` when the
   317	  submission's `status == "error"`; unchanged from `"starting"`
   318	  when the submission's `status == "eval_error"` (see §4.4 above
   319	  for the terminal-retry case).
   320	- `metrics` — set to the submission's `metrics` when

   504	        at ``expected_old_sha`` and raises :class:`GitError` otherwise.
   505	        """
   506	        args = ["update-ref", refname, new_sha]
   507	        if expected_old_sha is not None:
   508	            args.append(expected_old_sha)
   509	        self._run(args)
   510	
   511	    def delete_ref(self, refname: str, *, expected_old_sha: str | None = None) -> None:
   512	        """Delete a ref, optionally guarded by ``expected_old_sha``."""
   513	        args = ["update-ref", "-d", refname]
   514	        if expected_old_sha is not None:
   515	            args.append(expected_old_sha)
   516	        self._run(args)
   517	
   518	    # --- branches & worktrees --------------------------------------------
   519	
   520	    def create_branch(self, branch_name: str, start_point: str) -> None:

   522	        self._run(["branch", branch_name, start_point])
   523	
   524	    def delete_branch(self, branch_name: str) -> None:

   527	
   528	    def current_branch(self) -> str | None:

   534

1	# Phase 7b — Integrator flow (`eden_git.integrator`)
     2	
     3	## Goal
     4	
     5	Ship the integrator that composes the Phase 7a `GitRepo` subprocess
     6	wrapper with the Phase 6 `Store` to produce canonical `trial/*` commits
     7	per [`spec/v0/06-integrator.md`](../../spec/v0/06-integrator.md).
     8	Given a `success` trial with a recorded `commit_sha`, the integrator
     9	produces a single-commit squash whose tree is the worker-tip tree plus
    10	exactly the eval-manifest file at `.eden/trials/<trial_id>/eval.json`,
    11	writes the `trial/*` ref, writes `trial_commit_sha` on the trial, and
    12	appends `trial.integrated` — atomically with respect to in-process
    13	observers.
    14	
    15	See [`docs/roadmap.md`](../roadmap.md) §Phase 7 for context. Phase 7a
    16	is shipped (PR #12, merged `0fd58bd`).
    17	
    18	## Scope
    19	
    20	### Files to create
    21	
    22	| File | Purpose |
    23	|---|---|
    24	| `reference/packages/eden-git/src/eden_git/integrator.py` | `Integrator` class + `IntegratorError` hierarchy |
    25	| `reference/packages/eden-git/src/eden_git/_manifest.py` | Eval-manifest builder (§4.2) — separated for testability |
    26	| `reference/packages/eden-git/tests/test_integrator.py` | Success + rejection + idempotency + atomicity tests |
    27	| `reference/packages/eden-git/tests/test_manifest.py` | Eval-manifest shape tests |
    28	
    29	### Files to modify
    30	
    31	| File | Change |
    32	|---|---|
    33	| `reference/packages/eden-git/pyproject.toml` | Add `eden-contracts` + `eden-storage` workspace dependencies |
    34	| `reference/packages/eden-git/src/eden_git/__init__.py` | Re-export `Integrator`, `IntegratorError`, subclasses |
    35	| `reference/packages/eden-git/README.md` | Integrator surface + §3.2 / §3.4 contract notes |
    36	| `CLAUDE.md` (and symlink `AGENTS.md`) | Phase-7b completion paragraph |
    37	| `docs/roadmap.md` | Mark 7b complete |
    38	
    39	## API surface
    40	
    41	```python
    42	class Integrator:
    43	    def __init__(
    44	        self,
    45	        *,
    46	        store: Store,
    47	        repo: GitRepo,
    48	        author: Identity,
    49	        committer: Identity | None = None,
    50	        clock: Callable[[], datetime] | None = None,
    51	    ) -> None: ...
    52	
    53	    def integrate(self, trial_id: str) -> IntegrationResult: ...
    54	```
    55	
    56	- `store` supplies trial reads + proposal lookup (for `slug`) + the
    57	  composite `integrate_trial` commit.
    58	- `repo` is the experiment's git repository (bare or non-bare). The
    59	  integrator writes `trial/*` refs here.
    60	- `author`/`committer` stamp the squash commit (§6 leaves identity to
    61	  the implementation).
    62	- `clock` is injected for deterministic timestamps in tests; defaults
    63	  to `datetime.now(tz=UTC)`.
    64	
    65	```python
    66	@dataclass(frozen=True)
    67	class IntegrationResult:
    68	    trial_id: str
    69	    trial_commit_sha: str
    70	    branch: str            # e.g. "trial/<trial_id>-<slug>"
    71	    already_integrated: bool  # True on idempotent no-op path (§5.3)
    72	```
    73	
    74	### Error hierarchy
    75	
    76	```python
    77	class IntegratorError(RuntimeError): ...                 # base
    78	class NotReadyForIntegration(IntegratorError): ...       # §2: status != success, commit_sha absent, etc.
    79	class ReachabilityViolation(IntegratorError): ...        # §1.4
    80	class EvalManifestPathCollision(IntegratorError): ...    # §3.2: worker carried a file at the manifest path
    81	class CorruptIntegrationState(IntegratorError): ...      # §5.3: trial_commit_sha set but ref missing / tree disagrees
    82	class AtomicityViolation(IntegratorError): ...           # §3.4: compensating rollback itself failed
    83	```
    84	
    85	`IntegratorError` wraps `GitError` / `StorageError` only when the
    86	underlying operation should not be retried as-is. Otherwise those raise
    87	through unchanged so callers see the root cause.
    88	
    89	## Algorithm
    90	
    91	Executed in order, with the rollback rules documented in §3.4.
    92	
    93	1. **Load trial.** `trial = store.read_trial(trial_id)`. Raise
    94	   `NotFound` through.
    95	2. **Idempotency check (§5.3).**
    96	   - If `trial.trial_commit_sha is not None`:
    97	     - Resolve the SHA on the `trial/*` ref. If the ref exists, points
    98	       at the recorded SHA, and the commit's tree satisfies §3.2 (tree
    99	       derives from `trial.commit_sha`'s tree plus exactly the manifest
   100	       path, manifest blob matches §4.2), return
   101	       `IntegrationResult(..., already_integrated=True)`.
   102	     - Otherwise raise `CorruptIntegrationState`.
   103	3. **Promotion preconditions (§2).** Verify:
   104	   - `trial.status == "success"` — else `NotReadyForIntegration`.
   105	   - `trial.commit_sha is not None` — else `NotReadyForIntegration`.
   106	   - `trial.branch is not None` and the branch resolves to
   107	     `trial.commit_sha` (§2 second bullet). Raise
   108	     `NotReadyForIntegration` if the branch does not exist or resolves
   109	     to a different SHA.
   110	4. **Proposal lookup.** `proposal = store.read_proposal(trial.proposal_id)`.
   111	   The branch name uses `proposal.slug` per §3.1.
   112	5. **Reachability check (§1.4).** For each parent in
   113	   `trial.parent_commits`, verify
   114	   `repo.is_ancestor(parent, trial.commit_sha)`. On any failure raise
   115	   `ReachabilityViolation`. This defends against a work branch whose
   116	   tip does not descend from the declared parents.
   117	6. **Manifest-path collision check (§3.2).** If
   118	   `repo.tree_entry_exists(trial.commit_sha,
   119	   f".eden/trials/{trial.trial_id}/eval.json")` returns true, raise
   120	   `EvalManifestPathCollision`. The integrator MUST NOT overwrite.
   121	7. **Build squash tree.**
   122	   1. Build the manifest JSON bytes per §4.2 via `_manifest.py`.
   123	   2. `blob_sha = repo.write_blob(manifest_bytes)`.
   124	   3. `worker_tree = repo.commit_tree_sha(trial.commit_sha)`.
   125	   4. `tree_sha = repo.write_tree_with_file(worker_tree,
   126	      f".eden/trials/{trial.trial_id}/eval.json", blob_sha,
   127	      mode="100644")`. (`write_tree_with_file` already rejects on
   128	      nested-path collisions — belt-and-braces behind step 6.)
   129	8. **Commit object.**
   130	   - `message = f"trial: {trial.trial_id} {proposal.slug}\n"` (§3.3).
   131	   - `commit_sha = repo.commit_tree(tree_sha,
   132	     parents=list(trial.parent_commits), message=message,
   133	     author=self.author, committer=self.committer or self.author,
   134	     author_date=..., committer_date=...)`.
   135	   - Timestamps derive from `self.clock()`; tests inject a fixed clock.
   136	9. **Create `trial/*` ref.**
   137	   `branch = f"trial/{trial.trial_id}-{proposal.slug}"`
   138	   `repo.create_ref(f"refs/heads/{branch}", commit_sha)` (CAS with
   139	   zero-oid — fails if the ref already exists).
   140	10. **Atomic store write.**
   141	    `store.integrate_trial(trial.trial_id, commit_sha)`. If this raises:
   142	    `repo.delete_ref(f"refs/heads/{branch}",
   143	    expected_old_sha=commit_sha)` to compensate. Then re-raise the
   144	    original error. If `delete_ref` itself raises, wrap both errors in
   145	    `AtomicityViolation` — this is the one case where operator
   146	    intervention is required (§3.4).
   147	11. **Return** `IntegrationResult(..., already_integrated=False)`.
   148	
   149	### Why this ordering for atomicity (§3.4)
   150	
   151	Three observable artifacts: the git ref, the `trial_commit_sha` field,
   152	and the `trial.integrated` event. The spec requires a reader of any
   153	one to observe the other two.
   154	
   155	- **[write commit object, create ref, store.integrate_trial]** with
   156	  compensating ref-delete is the chosen order. Rationale:
   157	  - Writing the commit object (step 8) is content-addressed; the
   158	    object exists but nothing points at it. No reader can observe it
   159	    via a ref. It is GC-safe (`git gc` cleans unreachable objects).
   160	  - `create_ref` with zero-oid is itself a CAS; if concurrent writers
   161	    attempt the same branch, only one wins. This provides the Phase
   162	    7b single-writer discipline mechanically on top of the §1.2
   163	    sole-integrator rule.
   164	  - The store write (`integrate_trial`) is the atomic step that lands
   165	    the field + event together.
   166	  - If the store write fails, `delete_ref` with expected-old-sha
   167	    rolls back the ref without touching the committed object.
   168	- **Alternate orderings rejected:**
   169	  - `[store.integrate_trial, create_ref]`: if ref-create fails, the
   170	    store already committed both field and event. The store has no
   171	    "undo event" path — events are append-only per chapter 5. The
   172	    spec-violating state (field set, event written, ref absent) would
   173	    require operator intervention, strictly worse than the chosen
   174	    order's "ref set, field absent, event absent" intermediate state.
   175	  - Outbox pattern: deferred to Phase 8 when cross-process atomicity
   176	    matters. In-process, the synchronous execution of the three steps
   177	    within a single Python call means no intermediate state is
   178	    observable by other callers of the same `Store` / `GitRepo`
   179	    instance.
   180	
   181	### What the integrator does NOT do
   182	
   183	- **Does not re-validate metrics** against the experiment's
   184	  `metrics_schema` (§2, §5.1 permit but do not require this). The
   185	  orchestrator's `evaluate` terminal already enforces schema
   186	  conformance via `_validate_metrics` in `eden-storage/_base.py`;
   187	  re-validating at integration time would be defense-in-depth against
   188	  store corruption that is out of scope for the reference impl.
   189	  Documented as a deliberate non-goal.
   190	- **Does not observe events or poll.** The integrator is a passive
   191	  function: callers drive `integrate(trial_id)` when they decide.
   192	  The dispatch loop (`eden_dispatch.driver`) already has the
   193	  `_promote_successful_trials` scheduler; it will be updated to call
   194	  `Integrator.integrate(trial_id)` in place of its current
   195	  placeholder `integrator_commit_factory` hook.
   196	- **Does not manage work-branch retention** (§1.3). The integrator
   197	  neither deletes nor renames `work/*` branches; that is a deployment
   198	  concern.
   199	- **Does not populate optional manifest fields** `evaluator` or
   200	  `artifacts` (§4.3, §4.4). The v0 role contracts in `03-roles.md`
   201	  §4.4 do not surface evaluator identity or per-file artifact
   202	  inventory in the evaluator's submission shape; when role-bindings
   203	  add that metadata (Phase 10's LLM evaluator is the likely source)
   204	  the integrator grows two optional `Callable` hooks. Keeping the
   205	  shape minimal now avoids inventing a submission field ahead of
   206	  spec demand.
   207	
   208	## Eval-manifest builder (`_manifest.py`)
   209	
   210	Separate module so manifest shape has its own test target. Signature:
   211	
   212	```python
   213	def build_manifest(trial: Trial) -> bytes: ...
   214	```
   215	
   216	Builds a JSON object with the required fields from §4.2 (sourced
   217	directly from the `Trial` model). Emits the optional `description`
   218	and `artifacts_uri` only when present on the trial. Produces UTF-8
   219	bytes with `sort_keys=True` + `indent=2` + trailing newline so the
   220	blob is byte-stable across repeated promotions for idempotency check
   221	in step 2.
   222	
   223	- Required fields copied verbatim from the trial: `trial_id`,
   224	  `proposal_id`, `commit_sha`, `parent_commits`, `metrics`,
   225	  `completed_at`.
   226	- Optional `artifacts_uri` and `description` copied only if present.
   227	- No synthesis. No transformation. §4.2 closing paragraph: "a
   228	  conforming integrator MUST NOT synthesize or transform
   229	  required-field values."
   230	- Raises `ValueError` if any required trial field is `None`. Surfaces
   231	  as `NotReadyForIntegration` at the integrator layer (the conditions
   232	  that prevent those fields from being set are the same as §2's
   233	  preconditions; this is a guard, not a rewrite).
   234	
   235	## Updating `eden-dispatch` driver
   236	
   237	The driver currently accepts an `integrator_commit_factory:
   238	Callable[[str], str] | None` that returns a fake commit SHA. Phase 7b
   239	replaces this with a real `Integrator` call:
   240	
   241	```python
   242	def run_experiment(
   243	    ...,
   244	    integrator: Integrator | None = None,
   245	) -> None:
   246	```
   247	
   248	`_promote_successful_trials` becomes:
   249	
   250	```python
   251	def _promote_successful_trials(store: Store, integrator: Integrator) -> bool:
   252	    progress = False
   253	    for trial in store.list_trials(status="success"):
   254	        if trial.trial_commit_sha is not None:
   255	            continue
   256	        integrator.integrate(trial.trial_id)
   257	        progress = True
   258	    return progress
   259	```
   260	
   261	The `integrator_commit_factory` parameter is removed entirely — it
   262	was a Phase 5 placeholder, not a supported surface. Driver tests
   263	either supply a real `Integrator` backed by a test `GitRepo` or omit
   264	the integrator argument and assert trials remain at `status=success`
   265	with `trial_commit_sha=None`.
   266	
   267	## Tests
   268	
   269	### `test_manifest.py`
   270	
   271	- Required-field shape: builds manifest from a fully populated trial,
   272	  asserts every §4.2 required key + value equal to trial field.
   273	- Optional fields: omitted when absent, present when set.
   274	- Byte stability: two calls on the same trial produce identical
   275	  bytes (load-bearing for idempotency in `test_integrator.py`).
   276	- Rejects when any required field is `None` (trial with no
   277	  `commit_sha` / `metrics` / `completed_at`).
   278	
   279	### `test_integrator.py`
   280	
   281	Set up with a non-bare `GitRepo`, an `InMemoryStore`, a real proposal
   282	and trial seeded into the store, and a real worker branch at
   283	`trial.commit_sha` that descends from `trial.parent_commits`.
   284	
   285	**Success path:**
   286	
   287	- `integrate(trial_id)` returns `already_integrated=False`.
   288	- `refs/heads/trial/<trial_id>-<slug>` exists and resolves to a
   289	  commit whose parents are `trial.parent_commits` in order.
   290	- Commit subject line is `trial: <trial_id> <slug>`.
   291	- Commit tree's entry at `.eden/trials/<trial_id>/eval.json` is a
   292	  blob matching `build_manifest(trial)`.
   293	- Every other tree entry equals the corresponding entry under
   294	  `trial.commit_sha`'s tree (no drift, no drops).
   295	- `store.read_trial(trial_id).trial_commit_sha == result.trial_commit_sha`.
   296	- `store.events()` contains one `trial.integrated` event referencing
   297	  the trial + sha.
   298	
   299	**Idempotent replay (§5.3 bullet 1):**
   300	
   301	- Call `integrate(trial_id)` twice. Second call returns
   302	  `already_integrated=True` with the same SHA. No new ref write, no
   303	  new event. Assert len(events) equals expected + 1 total (not + 2).
   304	
   305	**Corrupt state (§5.3 bullet 2):**
   306	
   307	- Trial has `trial_commit_sha` set but ref does not exist → raises
   308	  `CorruptIntegrationState`, no side effects.
   309	- Trial has `trial_commit_sha` set and ref exists but tree does not
   310	  satisfy §3.2 (manifest missing or extra file) →
   311	  `CorruptIntegrationState`.
   312	
   313	**Reachability rejection (§1.4):**
   314	
   315	- Seed a trial whose `commit_sha` is unreachable from its
   316	  `parent_commits` (e.g. by rewriting `commit_sha` to point to a
   317	  separate commit chain). `integrate(trial_id)` raises
   318	  `ReachabilityViolation`. Assert no ref created, no
   319	  `trial_commit_sha` written, no `trial.integrated` event.
   320	
   321	**Manifest-path collision (§3.2):**
   322	
   323	- Seed a worker branch whose tree already contains
   324	  `.eden/trials/<trial_id>/eval.json`. Raise
   325	  `EvalManifestPathCollision`. No side effects.
   326	
   327	**Precondition failures (§2):**
   328	
   329	- `status=starting`, `status=error`, `status=eval_error` each raise
   330	  `NotReadyForIntegration`. No ref, no field, no event.
   331	- `commit_sha=None` → `NotReadyForIntegration`.
   332	- `branch` resolves to a different SHA → `NotReadyForIntegration`.
   333	
   334	**Atomic rollback (§3.4):**
   335	
   336	- Monkey-patch `store.integrate_trial` to raise. Run
   337	  `integrate(trial_id)`. Assert:
   338	  - The `trial/*` ref was deleted (compensating write).
   339	  - The original `store.integrate_trial` exception propagates through
   340	    `Integrator.integrate`.
   341	  - Trial `trial_commit_sha` remains `None`.
   342	  - No `trial.integrated` event appears.
   343	  - The commit object is still in the object DB (orphan; GC will
   344	    clean it). Verified via `repo.commit_exists(sha)` returning True.
   345	- Monkey-patch both `store.integrate_trial` and `repo.delete_ref` to
   346	  raise. Assert `AtomicityViolation` surfaces, wrapping both errors.
   347	
   348	**Commit identity:**
   349	
   350	- Custom `author` / `committer` / `clock` are honored. Commit has
   351	  `commit.gpgsign=false` (inherited from `GitRepo`).
   352	- Default `committer = author` when only `author` is supplied.
   353	
   354	**Multi-parent proposal:**
   355	
   356	- A trial with two `parent_commits` produces a commit with two
   357	  parents in order. (§3.2 supports multi-parent squash.)
   358

   263	Implementations MAY include additional informational fields beyond
   264	those listed; consumers MUST tolerate them.
   265	
   266	### 4.3 `evaluator` shape (optional)
   267	
   268	When present, the `evaluator` object has the form:
   269	
   270	```json
   271	{
   272	  "name": "<implementation-chosen identifier for the evaluator>",
   273	  "version": "<version string the evaluator presented at submission>"
   274	}
   275	```
   276	
   277	Both fields are required when the `evaluator` object itself is
   278	present. `name` and `version` together identify *which* evaluator
   279	measured the trial. Because v0 does not surface evaluator identity
   280	through [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §4.4, this field is optional:
   281	populate it when a role-binding or deployment convention carries
   282	evaluator metadata; omit it otherwise.
   283	
   284	### 4.4 `artifacts` shape (optional)
   285	
   286	When present, `artifacts` is a JSON object whose values describe
   287	per-file metadata for individual artifacts referenced from the trial
   288	(commonly the files under the evaluator's `artifacts_uri`). Each
   289	entry has the form:
   290	
   291	```json
   292	{
   293	  "uri": "<URI of the specific file>",
   294	  "sha256": "<hex sha256 of the file's byte content>",
   295	  "bytes": <non-negative integer>
   296	}
   297	```
   298	
   299	Keys are operator-chosen short labels (e.g. `"log"`, `"output.csv"`)
   300	and MUST be unique within the manifest. This inventory is **optional
   301	in v0** because [`03-roles.md`](03-roles.md) §4.4 does not require
   302	an evaluator to enumerate per-file hashes; a deployment whose role-
   303	binding surfaces that inventory MAY populate this field, and
   304	downstream consumers SHOULD prefer the inventory (when present) to
   305	blind re-fetches of `artifacts_uri` for reproducibility checks.
   306	
   307	### 4.5 Manifest immutability
   308	
   309	Because the eval manifest lives inside the `trial/*` commit's tree,
   310	it inherits the immutability of the `trial/*` branch (§1.2): once
   311	written, it MUST NOT be rewritten. A conforming implementation that
   312	discovers a manifest error after the fact MAY record corrective
   313	state outside git (operator-level event, separate annotation store)
   314	but MUST NOT mutate the `trial/*` commit.
   315	
   316	## 5. Failure modes
   317	
   318	### 5.1 Metrics-schema violation at promotion time
   319	
   320	If the integrator re-validates metrics (per §2) and finds them in
   321	violation of the experiment's `metrics_schema`, the integrator MUST
   322	NOT produce a `trial/*` commit, MUST NOT write `trial_commit_sha`,
   323	and MUST NOT append `trial.integrated`. It MUST surface the problem
   324	through an implementation-defined operator channel; since the trial's
   325	`status` was written to `success` by the orchestrator at the evaluate
   326	terminal, the promotion-side rejection is a protocol-level drift
   327	between the two that an operator MUST resolve. A conforming
   328	orchestrator makes this case rare by enforcing schema conformance at
   329	the evaluate terminal.
   330	
   331	### 5.2 Partial git write
   332	
   333	If the underlying git ref write appears to succeed but later fails
   334	durability (crashed filesystem, partial replication), the
   335	integrator's atomicity contract (§3.4) is violated. Implementations
   336	MUST ensure ref durability before considering a promotion complete;
   337	conforming storage requirements are pinned in
   338	[`08-storage.md`](08-storage.md) §5 (artifact store) and §3
   339	(durability semantics applied at the git layer by the integrator).
   340	
   341	### 5.3 Repeat promotion
   342	
   343	If the integrator is invoked twice for the same trial (replay of a
   344	`trial.succeeded` event, operator retry), it MUST be idempotent:
   345	
   346	- If the trial already has `trial_commit_sha` set and that SHA
   347	  resolves to a `trial/*` commit whose tree satisfies §3.2 (the
   348	  worker-tip tree with exactly the eval-manifest path added, no other
   349	  path changes) and whose eval manifest matches §4, the second
   350	  invocation MUST be a no-op (no new ref, no new event).
   351	- If the trial already has `trial_commit_sha` set but the recorded
   352	  SHA does not resolve or disagrees with the trial, the state is
   353	  corrupt; the integrator MUST NOT silently overwrite. An operator
   354	  channel per §5.1 applies.
   355	
   356	## 6. Implementation latitude
   357	
   358	The protocol leaves to implementations:
   359	
   360	- The git host (local bare repo, Gitea, GitHub, etc.) and the
   361	  transport used to write `trial/*` refs.
   362	- The retention policy for `work/*` branches after promotion.
   363	- The exact author/committer identity stamped on the integrator's
   364	  commits.
   365	- The mechanism of atomicity in §3.4 — outbox, store-level
   366	  transaction, compensating writes.
   367	
   368	What the protocol does **not** leave to implementations:
   369	
   370	- The three-namespace topology and sole-integrator rule (§1).
   371	- The single-commit squash shape and the tree rule in §3.2
   372	  (worker-tip tree plus exactly the eval-manifest path, no other
   373	  path changes).
   374	- The commit-message format (§3.3).
   375	- The eval-manifest path and required fields (§4).
   376	- The atomic-three invariant (§3.4).

item.text:
**1. Missing Context**

Assessment: mostly well-scoped, but one missing dependency is central enough that it should be resolved before implementation.

- `Major:` The plan never defines where the integrator gets the experiment’s `metrics_schema` or an equivalent validator. The proposed surface only passes `Store` and `GitRepo`, and `Store` does not expose the schema today, so a reader cannot see how the implementation would satisfy the promotion contract in the integrator spec. See [eden-phase-7b-integrator.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-7b-integrator.md:41), [protocol.py](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/reference/packages/eden-storage/src/eden_storage/protocol.py:63), and [06-integrator.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/06-integrator.md:104).

**2. Feasibility**

Assessment: significant concerns here. I would stop at this level before spending time on alternatives, completeness, or edge cases, because the current plan does not yet describe a conforming implementation.

- `Critical:` The atomicity design is weaker than the spec it cites. The plan explicitly scopes the guarantee to “in-process observers” and accepts a transient `trial/*` ref before the store write/event append, but the spec requires that a reader of any one artifact observe the other two, and requires rollback of already-performed steps on failure. As written, this is not just an implementation detail; it is a different contract. See [eden-phase-7b-integrator.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-7b-integrator.md:11), [eden-phase-7b-integrator.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-7b-integrator.md:149), and [06-integrator.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/06-integrator.md:192).

- `Critical:` The plan explicitly treats metrics validation at promotion time as optional/non-goal, but the normative text is stronger than that framing. `06-integrator.md` says the integrator “MUST NOT promote” a trial whose metrics do not validate; the plan currently argues that prior store validation is enough and documents revalidation out of scope. If that is the intended implementation latitude, the plan needs to justify it much more carefully or change the design to enforce it directly. See [eden-phase-7b-integrator.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-7b-integrator.md:181) and [06-integrator.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/06-integrator.md:104).

- `Major:` The promotion precondition is stricter than the spec. The plan requires `trial.branch` to resolve exactly to `trial.commit_sha`, while the spec only requires `commit_sha` to resolve to a commit on the branch. That could reject a trial that is still spec-valid under the documented trigger. See [eden-phase-7b-integrator.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/docs/plans/eden-phase-7b-integrator.md:103) and [06-integrator.md](/Users/ericalt/Documents/eden/spec/v0/06-integrator.md:87).

Because the feasibility issues are foundational, I would skip alternatives, completeness, and edge-case review for this round.

**Overall Assessment**

The plan is thoughtful and clearly grounded in the current `eden-git` and `eden-storage` surfaces, but it is not ready to implement as the Phase 7b plan yet. The atomicity model and the promotion-time metrics contract both need to be reconciled with `spec/v0/06-integrator.md` first; until those are fixed, the plan describes a useful reference mechanism, not a clearly conforming implementation.
